listings.description:
Situated in the heart of Manhattan's coveted Gold Coast, Residence 5BC at 900 Park Avenue is a grand, sun-drenched three bedroom, three bathroom home boasting stunning views over Park Avenue. Originally architected by Annabelle Selldorf and recently updated in 2021, and beautifully laid out over approximately 1,890 square feet, this spectacular home boasts spacious rooms and a carefully considered separation of the private quarters from the entertaining spaces. 

A gracious foyer welcomes you into the home and leads directly into the corner great room with sweeping views over Park Avenue. The chef’s eat-in kitchen features a suite of top-of-the-line appliances by Viking, SubZero, Wolf, and Gaggenau, elegant soapstone counters as well as endless glass cabinetry, and can be enclosed via pocket doors. A full bathroom, ample closets and a Bosch washer and dryer round out the offerings in the entertaining wing.

Overlooking Park Avenue through a wall of windows, the palatial primary suite features floor to ceiling closets, a large walk-in closet, and an ensuite designed with Selldorf’s signature glass tiles, a large soapstone soaking tub, a walk-in shower with dual showerhead and a double vanity. A second bedroom features a walk-in closet and an ensuite with a combined soaking tub and shower. The third bedroom could be a guest room or reappropriated as a media room/den.

A full-service condominium building, The Park 900 boasts amenities and conveniences that include a 24-hour doorman and concierge staff, a newly renovated fitness center and residents’ lounge, a convenient building driveway and an in-house garage.

Central Park, renowned boutiques and restaurants, the Metropolitan Museum of Art, and top-rated public and private schools are all just moments away.

Corner Pre-War Residence on Madison Avenue in the heart of Carnegie Hill

Providing a wide corner footprint and impressive expanse along Madison Avenue, this vast approximately 3,000 square foot four-bedroom three and one half, right room residence offers a level of scale and proportion coupled with wonderful natural light and charming views that is increasingly difficult to find.

A proper entry gallery sets a clear sense of arrival and anchors a classic split-wing layout. Original pre-war details carry throughout, including beamed ceilings, steel casement windows, and original restored hardwood floors laid in herringbone and parquet patterns- timeless elements that define the home's character.

The living room is the defining space- a large corner room facing Madison Avenue and 88th Street with windows on two exposures including a dramatic bay window with a built-in seat overlooking the Avenue. A full wall of custom floor-to-ceiling bookshelves, softly illuminated and accessed by a rolling library ladder, creates a striking architectural moment while adding both presence and function. The room is bright, well-proportioned, and quietly impressive, with clean southeastern light that transitions to a softer, cooler feel by afternoon.

Nearly 50 feet of living and dining space creates a natural entertaining flow, with a gracious formal dining room that connects seamlessly to the windowed in eat kitchen. The kitchen is well laid out with stainless steel appliances and cabinets, generous counter space, and room for a proper breakfast table, enjoying a bright morning feel and lovely start of the day. The formal dining room comfortably seats ten for dinner and is a delightful space for hosting large and small gatherings. A discreet powder room is a highly coveted amenity for guest use.

The northern wing is connected via a wide, hallway that joins the public and private domains. Four generous bedrooms, three full baths and a service kitchen/pantry space ideally converted into a windowed laundry room define the private wing. The primary suite is massive- the former living room of the H line apartment and most generous- and filled with morning light. Ample space for the addition of a windowed dressing room define this vast suite.

Storage throughout is exceptional, with multiple closets lining the gallery and hallways. The entire home is graced with a timeless, subtle elegance. With the exception of one room, every single window faces the Avenue or Street- no compromised exposures.

Built in 1937 and designed by William M. Dowling, this 16-story Art Deco cooperative offers a full-service experience with a 24-hour doorman, resident superintendent, stunning planted roof deck, new fitness room, central laundry, and storage. The lobby is redolent of the Carlyle Hotel and is the epitome of elegance. The building has 98 units across 16 floors. 

Ideally located between Madison and Fifth Avenue, moments from Central Park and neighboring the Guggenheim Museum. Subletting permitted after three years of ownership, subject to board approval (two out of every four years).  

Temporary capital assessment of $1,070/month through October 2027 supporting major building improvements, including facade and hallway upgrades.  

Flip tax of 2% of the purchase price, payable by the purchaser. Max Financing = 50% of Purchase Price.

Perched on the 10th floor of this distinguished white-glove pre-war condominium, this beautifully appointed corner residence is bathed in natural light and offers a refined, contemporary aesthetic while preserving its classic architectural charm.

A gracious entrance gallery welcomes you into the home, immediately conveying a sense of scale and elegance, with soaring 10-foot ceilings and beautifully proportioned rooms throughout.

The expansive, light-filled corner living room anchors the residence, offering a sophisticated blend of timeless charm and modern comfort. Rich, dark-stained hardwood floors add depth and contrast, while large windows invite abundant natural light and provide open city views. 

Thoughtfully designed built-ins frame a charming fireplace, creating a warm and inviting focal point for both everyday living and entertaining.
Seamlessly connected to the living room, the gracious dining room provides an elegant setting for entertaining. A generous window fills the space with natural light, while integrated millwork and shelving offer both display and storage.

Privately situated in the eastern wing, both bedrooms enjoy open eastern and southern exposures, filling the spaces with soft natural light. Each bedroom features an ensuite bathroom and generous closet space.

The sleek, efficiently designed kitchen offers a clean, modern aesthetic. Custom white cabinetry provides abundant storage, beautifully complemented by dark stone countertops, and the space is equipped with a washer and dryer for added convenience.

525 Park Avenue is a premier 14-story pre-war condominium designed by the renowned architectural firm Schwartz & Gross and completed in 1915 in the Italian Palazzo style. Ideally situated on Park Avenue at 61st Street, in the heart of one of Manhattan’s most prestigious and sought-after neighborhoods, the building offers full-service white-glove amenities and a level of privacy rarely found on Park Avenue.

Residents enjoy immediate proximity to Central Park, as well as the city’s finest restaurants, cafés, boutiques, galleries, and world-class museums. The building is also notably flexible, permitting financing, LLC ownership, and purchases in trust.

There is a Working Capital Contribution equal to one month’s common charges and real estate taxes payable by the purchaser.

Apartment 10D is also available for sale and could be a wonderful opportunity for a buyer seeking additional space.

The Manhattan Residence of Robert A. M. Stern

A rare and distinguished offering, this exceptional two-bedroom residence was the private home of the late, renowned architect Robert A. M. Stern. Defined by grand scale, refined detail, and serene outlooks, the apartment is graced with soaring 11-foot ceilings and dramatic floor-to-ceiling French windows overlooking one of Manhattan's most beautiful private landscaped gardens.

A gracious 20-foot gallery creates a striking first impression and leads to an impressive 35-foot-long great room, where natural light pours in from three exposures. At its northern end, oversized French doors open onto a charming terrace-an idyllic setting for quiet moments or entertaining-enjoying tranquil garden views below.

Occupying the south wing, the primary suite is a peaceful retreat, featuring a generous dressing room and an expansive en-suite bath. With its lovely western exposures over the garden, the suite is both sunlit and pin-drop quiet.

Across the gallery, a handsome library or study-lined with custom floor-to-ceiling bookshelves-offers flexibility as a guest bedroom, complemented by an adjacent full bath.

The beautifully proportioned, 17-foot windowed kitchen is appointed with abundant counter space and top-tier appliances from Miele, Thermador, and Sub-Zero.

The Chatham, a distinguished white-glove condominium designed by Robert A. M. Stern, offers residents an exceptional level of service and amenities, including a full-time doorman, concierge, state-of-the-art fitness center, and on-site garage. Ideally located in the heart of the Upper East Side, the finest dining, shopping, and cultural institutions are just moments away.

Corner 2 bedroom w/ soaring ceilings
This sprawling, 1860 sqft residence features voluminous 19’ ceilings and frames panoramic views of the Manhattan skyline to the North and West through floor-to-ceiling windows. The refined primary suite is equally spectacular with a spa inspired en suite bath and generously proportioned wardrobe.

In an era of luxury, Elena Frampton’s interior creation gives way to the power of the unexpected. Tailored design is enriched with contrast and a layering of organic materials. A distinct spirit and style – together monumental, seductive and graciously proportioned with unparalleled, loft-like ceiling heights. Future-forward technology is woven throughout with high performance floor-to-ceiling windows, bespoke air systems and hydronic, radiant heated floors.

Perfectly framed to inspire, the residences atop The Leyton offer the pinnacle of viewing pleasure. Glass corner living spaces, expansive windows and private outdoor terraces offer residents an unparalleled perspective above the Upper East Side. Northern and Western vistas capture the verdant green canopy of Central Park while Eastern views offer dramatic sunsets over the East River. By evening, lights glitter from the beaux-arts era Queensboro Bridge.

Seamlessly connected to the lofty great room, the custom Poggenpohl kitchen evokes a clean, contemporary feeling. Rich personality is injected into the design scheme with polished marble countertops, sleek functionality and an integrated Gaggenau appliance suite. Every element of these gracious, well-appointed kitchens is curated for the culinary creative. The primary suite is thoughtfully oriented, ensuring dramatic sunrise to sunset exposures. Spacious walk-in wardrobes, an ensuite primary bathroom and radiant-heated floors throughout enhance the ambience of this haven. The distinctive powder room is lined with Aged Dark Nature slab walls and a marble mosaic floor, and accessorized with Fantini fixtures.

Sumptuous, out of the ordinary and dignified, The Leyton offers the luxury of comfort, up high. An extraordinary collection of 38 residences nestled in Manhattan’s epicenter, overlooking Central Park, the East River and surrounding cityscape. With a highly visible location off East 63rd Street, The Leyton artfully nods to its landmark neighbors. Architect Manuel Glas applies classic Deco design, creating a distinctive urbane addition with stunning views and outdoor spaces in one of the most premiere neighborhoods in New York.

Alexandra Champalimaud, revered for her palatial approach to designing the world’s most renowned hotels, crafted the two levels of amenity spaces at The Leyton. Inspired by the Upper East Side’s grande dame boutique hotels, The Leyton embeds a culture of service into every amenity experience beginning with the epically scaled 24-hour attended lobby.

Encompassing the 24th floor, the Brandy Room offers a collection of indoor and outdoor entertaining spaces with sprawling 270-degree vistas of the city skyline. The Living Room features a chalet fireplace surrounded by rich seating and warm lighting leading to a cocktail terrace with Central Park views. Just off the Living Room, the Dining Room offers a table for ten and fully outfitted butler’s pantry that can be reserved for private entertaining. Completing the Brandy Room experience, the sun drenched Solarium unfolds onto the Sun Terrace & Grille with East River views and an al fresco kitchen.

Designed for the ultimate personal fitness experience, the sunlit Studio pays mindful attention to detail and privacy. State of the art studio and conditioning equipment plus a dedicated stretching area provide and intimate centre for wellness.

PENTHOUSE E2103 | Manhattan House

RARE OFFERING - This triple-mint penthouse -  part of The Penthouse Collection in the coveted and prestigious Manhattan House Condominium is the definition of elevated luxury—offered for the first time since the sponsor sale. 

Masterfully designed by AD100 visionary Vicente Wolf, this exceptional home embodies quiet luxury at its highest level. Originally conceived as a two-bedroom, two-bath layout, it has been elegantly reimagined to feature a grand primary suite with an extra  bespoke dressing room— easily adaptable to a third bedroom or private office.

RESIDENCE HIGHLIGHTS:
Bathed in natural light & surrounded by Manhattan skyline views
Northern & Southern Exposures
Penthouses offer extra height 9 foot ceilings with integrated Lutron lighting system and recessed lighting. 
Custom millwork, built-ins, and exceptional dressing/closet space
Art Deco wood-burning fireplace with bespoke stainless steel surround
Spa-caliber primary bath with Waterworks soaking tub, Dornbracht fixtures, and custom bleached oak vanity
Designer kitchen with honed Basaltina countertops, custom oak & stainless cabinetry, and Wolf/Miele appliances 
In-Unit Washer/Dryer
Wide-plank rift-cut white oak floors with custom gray stain and Basaltina inlays
Motorized window treatments throughout
Offered unfurnished, or with curated art and furniture priced separately.
A storage unit is included in the sale! 

THE MANHATTAN HOUSE:
Iconic and Prestigious Full-Service Luxury Living
Grand glass-enclosed lobby with porte-cochère arrival
Private landscaped gardens—among the largest in Manhattan
24-hour doorman, concierge & resident manager
The Manhattan Club: ~10,000 sq ft rooftop lounge with sweeping skyline views
State-of-the-art fitness center & on-site parking
Valet, dry cleaning, playroom, bike storage & additional storage
Parking in the building

Offered unfurnished, or furnished with fine art and furniture priced separately.
A storage unit is included in the sale! 

INTERESTED IN THE MANHATTAN HOUSE? - CONTACT DEVIN & KELLY - ON & OFF MARKET OPPORTUNITIES!

Perfectly located on 72nd Street and Fifth Avenue, this splendid residence offers the rare and fortunate combination of gracefully-scaled pre-war sensibility and a highly sophisticated contemporary aesthetic.  Orchestrated by an Architectural Digest and NYT-featured designer, this meticulous and thoughtful renovation affords every modern convenience.

Accessed via a semi-private elevator landing, a stylish rectangular Gallery with a graphically-patterned marble floor and paired sculptural ceiling leads to the expansive Living Room/Dining room with very high ceilings and glorious open southern outlooks over the gorgeous landmarked townhouses along East 72nd Street and glimpses of Central Park.  Nearby, there is a pristine chef’s kitchen with quartzite counters, custom ash cabinetry, and a panoply of state-of-the-art Miele and Sub-Zero appliances.  An exquisite marble Powder Room and copious closets are accessible from the Galley.  

Beyond, the private quarters are comprised of a Primary Bedroom suite, replete with a spacious Dressing Room with thoughtfully-designed custom fittings and an ample and very glamorous marble Bathroom featuring a separate tub and walk-in shower.  There is one additional Bedroom, a handsome marble bathroom, and a convenient Laundry Room.  

Throughout, this perfectly-maintained, mint-condition apartment benefits from multiple-zone, remotely-operated HVAC, very high ceilings, impeccable new hardwood and marble floors, remote-control sheer and black-out shades, Lutron lighting and Sonos sound systems, and high-performance, acoustically-engineered windows. 
 
Originally conceived in 1919 by Fred F. French Company and modified to its current design in 1959, 910 Fifth Avenue is a coveted white-glove cooperative with top services and amenities, as well as an ideal location just moments from Central Park.  Impeccable staffing includes a full-time doorman, porters, and a highly-regarded resident manager.   Residents enjoy a wide host of amenities, including a private fitness center, bicycle and pram storage, and a generously-reduced rate at the on-site parking garage.  Please note that 910 Fifth Avenue is a pet-free building.

listings.description:
Now offering an elevated perspective, Residence 15B is a beautifully appointed, highest of it's kind, three-bedroom, two-and-a-half-bathroom home spanning approximately 1,700 square feet. 

Thoughtfully designed interiors are framed by elegant picture moldings and White Oak herringbone floors, while oversized, triple-glazed windows draw in abundant southern and northern light throughout the day. The expansive living and dining area opens to a private balcony overlooking Central Park-an inviting extension of the home perfect for morning coffee or evening unwinding.

The open kitchen is both refined and functional, featuring custom Scavolini cabinetry, Carrara marble countertops, a fully integrated suite of Gaggenau appliances, and Waterworks fixtures.The serene primary suite offers generous proportions, dual custom closets, and a spa-quality en-suite bath finished in white marble. Highlights include a Scavolini vanity, custom jacuzzi tub, Robern medicine cabinets, radiant heated floors, and a towel warmer for added comfort.

Two additional bedrooms enjoy bright southern exposures and share a beautifully finished bathroom with marble walls, mosaic tile floors, a Scavolini vanity, and Circa lighting. A striking powder room clad in Nero Marquina marble adds a sophisticated touch.

Modern comforts include a residence-specific air purification system and an in-unit Samsung washer and fully vented gas dryer. Residence comes with additional storage unit. 

Designed by Antonio Ventimiglia and S20M Milan, 126 East 86th Street is a boutique limestone condominium with just 28 residences, offering an intimate living experience complemented by full-service amenities. Residents enjoy an attended lobby, fitness center, spa with infrared and traditional saunas, library, children's playroom, music room, and a lounge with landscaped outdoor terrace and fireplaces.

Ideally located between Park and Lexington Avenues, moments from Central Park, premier dining, shopping, and multiple subway lines.

There is a current monthly assessment of $776.27 until September 2026.

At the distinguished corner of Park Avenue and East 78th Street, within the elegant prewar cooperative 875 Park Avenue, this extraordinary 4,000+ square-foot maisonette presents a rare opportunity to create a grand Upper East Side residence while enjoying a level of flexibility seldom found in Park Avenue cooperatives.

Currently configured as a live-work residence with a medical office, Apartment 1C/1D occupies an elevated position above street level with 70 feet of frontage on Park Avenue and offers the privacy and scale of a townhouse paired with the prestige and services of a full-service Park Avenue building. The property features its own private entrance directly on Park Avenue, providing discreet access in addition to the building's full-service lobby entrance.

Behind the doors lies a sprawling and highly adaptable floor plan capable of accommodating a variety of configurations. While the space is presently arranged to support a medical practice alongside residential quarters, the apartment may be reimagined as a magnificent full-floor residence with grand entertaining rooms, multiple bedrooms, a library, and expansive living and dining spaces befitting a classic Park Avenue lifestyle.

The maisonette's elevated position allows for excellent privacy while maintaining the scale and architectural presence associated with the finest prewar cooperatives. Generous proportions, abundant natural light, and flexible room divisions provide a blank canvas for architects and designers to craft a truly bespoke home.

Enhancing the offering further, two separate staff rooms are also available for purchase. These additional spaces offer the possibility to expand the primary residence or create dedicated staff accommodations and may potentially be combined with the main apartment, subject to board approval.

Opportunities of this scale and flexibility on Park Avenue are rare. A distinctive maisonette with private entry and remarkable adaptability, offering the chance to create a grand residence in one of the Upper East Side's most prestigious addresses.

OPEN HOUSES ARE BY APPOINTMENT ONLY. 

South-Facing Lenox Hill Townhouse with Private Elevator, Sunroom, and Flexible Mixed-Use Zoning

Welcome to 197 East 76th Street — a sun-filled, south-facing townhouse offering an exceptional combination of elegance, space, and versatility in the heart of Lenox Hill. Spanning five stories with a private elevator, this beautifully maintained home features a rare sunroom and rooftop terrace, creating an ideal urban sanctuary.

Currently configured as a mixed-use property, the ground floor hosts a high-end chocolate boutique with a separate street entrance, providing an excellent opportunity for income generation. The residential entry is discreetly positioned off-street and includes space for a bike or stroller. Live upstairs while collecting rent below, create a live-work setup, or easily convert the home to a single-family residence.

The second floor boasts a gracious full-floor living room with a dramatic decorative fireplace, custom built-ins, a walk-in closet, and a powder room — perfect for entertaining. The third floor features a chef’s kitchen with a Viking range, Sub-Zero refrigerator, Bosch dishwasher, and Miele washer/dryer. A pass-through opens into a refined dining room with a second decorative fireplace and four windows that flood the space with natural light.

On the fourth floor, you'll find two well-proportioned bedrooms, a full bath, and a shared walk-in closet. The fifth floor is dedicated to the expansive primary suite, offering two walk-in closets and an en-suite bathroom with a double vanity.

The crown jewel of the home is the top-floor sunroom, leading to a private rooftop terrace — an ideal spot for lounging or entertaining. This versatile space could also function as an additional bedroom suite, second living room, home office, or creative studio.

The lower level includes a half-bath, mechanicals, and ample storage for the retail tenant.

Additional highlights include:

Approx. 2,400+ buildable square feet of unused FAR (consult your architect)

Elevator access from floors two through six

Flexible zoning and excellent live-work potential

Prime Location: Nestled between Central Park and John Jay Park, this townhouse is surrounded by iconic Upper East Side destinations including The Carlyle, The Mark, and Madison Avenue boutiques. Just moments from the 6 and Q trains, M79 crosstown bus, and beloved neighborhood favorites like JG Melon, Seamore’s, Breads Bakery, and Van Leeuwen.

1 FREE PARKING LICENSE INCLUDED WITH THIS RESIDENCE FOR A LIMITED TIME

IMMEDIATE OCCUPANCY

Residence 31C is a corner three-bedroom, two-bathroom home with powder room that offers sweeping northern and western views over the Hudson River, Riverside and Sakura Parks, and the iconic spire of Riverside Church. Boasting over 1,600 square feet of finely detailed living space, this residence features a gracious corner living and dining room that adjoins both a covered outdoor loggia space and a windowed kitchen that can be elegantly concealed behind a solid wood pocket door. The west-facing primary bedroom suite incorporates a 5-fixture bathroom with deep soaking tub and large glass-enclosed walk-in shower, and a walk-in closet. Two additional bedrooms share this residence's spectacular views over the Hudson River, as well as a conveniently located hall bath.
Displaying a refined finish palette hand-selected by Robert A.M. Stern Architects, all residences at Claremont Hall include a custom-designed solid wood entrance door with polished chrome hardware, wide plank European white oak flooring, expansive, high-performance windows, and up to 10' ceilings.

Select residences feature generous bay picture windows and Gothic-inspired arched windows. Kitchens at Claremont Hall feature custom Italian cabinetry in soothing Classic Bianco or Modern Grigio lacquer, topped by Calacatta Laza quartz countertops and backed by a ceramic tile backsplash and a paneled appliance package from Bosch, with a custom range hood with integrated spice shelf. Paneled wine refrigerators are included in select residences.

Primary baths are finished with Arabescato Cervaiole marble and feature marble mosaic tile flooring and custom sconces in polished chrome. Each primary bath includes a RAMSA-designed Italian walnut vanity with Arabescato Cervaiole marble countertop, polished chrome fixtures by Kallista, custom designed mirrored medicine cabinet by Robern, and water closet fixtures by Toto.
Secondary baths are well appointed with marble mosaic tile flooring framed by Bianco Carrara border and ceramic tile surround, ceramic tile shower surround, custom vanities designed by RAMSA, Grigio Toscana marble countertops, and Kohler undermount sinks with polished chrome hardware from Purist by Kohler. Secondary baths also include oversized mirrored medicine cabinets with polished chrome frames by Robern, Kohler soaking tubs or showers with polished chrome Purist rain showerheads and hand-held sprayers by Kohler, and water closets by Toto.
Powder rooms offer five-inch-wide European white oak flooring with Gothic-inspired wainscoting, custom Italian vanities designed by RAMSA with Nero Marquina marble countertops, RAMSA-designed Central Park West Collection fixtures by Kallista, custom oversized mirrors by Robern, polished chrome sconces, and water closet fixtures by Toto. Select homes feature private outdoor loggias or terraces. Each residence conveniently features a washer and dryer by Bosch.

Claremont Hall's robust amenities span over three floors and include spaces for socializing, working, and relaxing. The experience is centered on the Refectory Pool, a grand historic dining hall transformed into a swimming and events space, featuring a 48' salt-water swimming pool. Claremont Hall is complete with a grand, double-height lobby, a Reading Room with Great Books Collection, a Gymnasium with Flex Space, the Little Castle playroom, media room, and a Residents' Lounge and Terrace overlooking tranquil Sakura Park. The building also features a concierge, handyman, porters, and a live-in resident manager, as well as a bicycle room, and the opportunity to purchase on-site parking and private storage.

FOR A LIMITED TIME, SPONSOR WILL INCLUDE 1 FREE PARKING LICENSE WITH THIS RESIDENCE. The complete offering terms are in an offering plan available from Sponsor. File No. CD-20-0302. Sponsor: Broadway Community Owner LLC, c/o Lendlease, 200 Park Avenue, 9th Floor, NY, NY 10166

Beautifully Renovated, 3,065 square foot, 5 Bedroom (Convertible 6 Bedroom) Condo on the Upper East Side for only $3.895m.

This designer home is an amazing value.  It's 1 of only 4 apartments for sale from East 65th to East 95th Street with at least 5 Bedrooms, priced under $4.2m, (and it's the only Condo)!

You will love this sprawling 9 room home featuring a Double-Wide Sunken Living Room, Formal Dining Room, Eat-in-Kitchen, a Den, 5 Bedrooms and 4 Bathrooms.

Entertaining will be a pleasure in your Double-wide Living Room with nearly 10' Ceilings and Huge Windows.

Chefs will be wowed by this Top-of-the-Line Scavolini Kitchen.  It features a side-by-side 36" Sub-Zero Refrigerator and Freezer, Miele Double Oven, Microwave & Dishwasher, Gaggenau 5-Burner Gas Cooktop, 60 Bottle Subzero Wine Refrigerator, Quartz Countertops and Backsplash, Tons of Counter Space, a Breakfast Bar, 2 Pantries and Slow-Closing Drawers.

Hosting dinner parties of 20 will be a breeze in your extra-large Dining Room.

Relax and Recharge in your versatile Den that can also become a 6th Bedroom if you would like.

The Primary Bedroom is huge and boasts one of the largest walk-in closets you can find.   Additional closets, a Home Office area, and an En-Suite Bath complete this sanctuary.  Luxuriate in your Spa-like Primary Bath, with Floor-to-Ceiling Marble, Double Sinks, and a combination Shower/Deep Soaking Tub.

There are Four additional King-sized Bedrooms with ample storage and beautifully outfitted closets. 

All Four Bathrooms are New Development quality and feature Waterworks Marble and high-end fixtures from Lacava, Kohler, Duravit, among others. 

A Washer & Dryer, High Ceilings, Oversized Windows, Oak Hardwood Floors, Solid Core Doors, Crown and Baseboard Moldings, an Entry Foyer, and a Gallery complete this stunning UES home.  

*Please contact us for more details about this special apartment or to schedule a showing.

The Wakefield is an elegant Full-Service Condo with 24-Hour Doorman, Parking Garage, renovated hallways, new elevators, Storage For Rent, Bike Room and Laundry Room. This is a NON smoking building.

Great Upper East Side Location near Carl Schurz Park, the new 2nd Ave. Subway (83rd St. entrance), the 6 train on 77th St. and the 4 & 5 Express train on 86th St. Fantastic restaurants & amazing supermarkets line the neighborhood including Agata & Valentina, Citarella, Fairway, Whole Foods and Eli's.

THE APARTMENT 

Designed in 1923 by Rosario Candela-the preeminent architect of New York's great apartment houses-1105 Park Avenue, Apartment 11B is a quintessential example of his enduring legacy of proportion, elegance, and quiet grandeur. It also reflects why discerning buyers will often wait years for the opportunity to acquire a Candela residence in estate condition-rather than inherit the compromises of a prior renovation.

Offered for the first time in decades, this high-floor home is bathed in natural light, with four principal rooms (and two bathrooms) enjoying open views over Park Avenue- an exposure that has long made the "B" line particularly prized.

The layout is both classic and highly functional. A signature 29-foot gallery-one of Candela's most celebrated design elements-creates a striking entrance and an ideal setting for art and collections.
Three generously scaled bedrooms are privately set apart from the entertaining rooms, which feel truly expansive with high ceilings, refined architectural detail, and a wood-burning fireplace. A rarely found 44-foot expanse from living room to dining room enhances both scale and flow.

The rear of the apartment offers meaningful flexibility, including the opportunity to reimagine the kitchen and adjoining staff room into a substantial eat-in kitchen tailored to today's lifestyle. A second generously sized staff room allows for a variety of additional uses, whether as a home office, expanded service area, or a well-appointed laundry room.

THE BUILDING 

1105 Park Avenue is one of Carnegie Hill's most distinguished prewar cooperatives, occupying a prime corner at 89th Street just moments from Central Park, Museum Mile, and the boutiques and restaurants of Madison Avenue, as well as a concentration of the city's finest schools.

The building offers the level of service and discretion expected of a classic Park Avenue address, with full-time doorman and concierge staff and a live-in resident manager. Amenities include a large fitness center, bicycle room, and private storage for each residence.

Originally constructed in 1923 and intimate in scale, the cooperative comprises just 57 residences across 14 floors. It permits financing of up to 50% and welcomes pets with Board approval. A 2% flip tax is paid by the purchaser. The building is managed by Brown Harris Stevens.

PLEASE INQUIRE ABOUT OUR PARKING INCENTIVE FOR THIS RESIDENCE

IMMEDIATE OCCUPANCY

Spanning 1,741 square feet of beautifully crafted interior and 244sf of exterior living space, residence 35C is a two-bedroom, two-bathroom home with powder room that offers a blend of indoor and outdoor living. This home features a spacious living and dining room framed by two terraces, with breathtaking views: one showcases sweeping panoramas of the Hudson River, George Washington Bridge, and Riverside and Sakura parks, while the other enjoys open city and Morningside Park vistas. The gracious primary bedroom suite includes a luxurious five-fixture bathroom with a deep soaking tub, glass-enclosed walk-in shower, and ample closet space. The second bedroom, complete with ensuite bathroom, has abundant natural light and views.

Residence 35C is perfectly suited for entertaining or relaxing, combining refined and spacious interiors with extraordinary outdoor spaces for an enlightened living experience. Displaying a refined finish palette hand-selected by Robert A.M. Stern Architects, all residences at Claremont Hall include a custom-designed solid wood entrance door with polished chrome hardware, wide plank European white oak flooring, expansive, high-performance windows, and up to 10" ceilings. Select residences feature generous bay picture windows and Gothic-inspired arched windows. Kitchens at Claremont Hall feature custom Italian cabinetry in soothing Classic Bianco or Modern Grigio lacquer, topped by Calacatta Laza quartz countertops and backed by a ceramic tile backsplash and a paneled appliance package from Bosch, with a custom range hood with integrated spice shelf. Paneled wine refrigerators are included in select residences.

Each primary bath includes a RAMSA-designed Italian walnut vanity with Arabescato Cervaiole marble countertop, polished chrome fixtures by Kallista, custom designed mirrored medicine cabinet by Robern, and water closet fixtures by Toto.

Secondary baths are well appointed with marble mosaic tile flooring framed by Bianco Carrara border and ceramic tile surround, ceramic tile shower surround, custom vanities designed by RAMSA, Grigio Toscana marble countertops, and Kohler undermount sinks with polished chrome hardware from Purist by Kohler. Living/Dining Room: Step through the gracious entry foyer — finished in Venetian plaster — into a showstopping great room framed by sweeping views of Central Park, the Vanderbilt Gate, and the Conservatory Gardens. Soaring 9-foot beamed ceilings, plaster crown moldings, solid oak floors, and oversized tilt-and-turn windows make this space perfect for grand entertaining or quiet evenings above the treetops.

Kitchen: The open chef's kitchen features Caesarstone countertops, custom Italian cabinetry, and a powerhouse lineup of Bertazzoni, Viking, and Miele appliances. Dornbracht fixtures, a garbage disposer, and recessed LED lighting round out this sleek, functional space.

Primary Suite: A private sanctuary in its own south wing with direct park views and two custom walk-in closets. The extraordinary primary bath is clad in Bianco Dolomiti marble with radiant heated floors, a Kohler cast-iron Jacuzzi soaking tub, separate walk-in shower, and the legendary Kohler Numi toilet — recently serviced and unique to this model apartment. A custom Robern mirror cabinet with built-in television and music system adds another layer of luxury.

Second Bedroom / Home Office: A generous room with direct Central Park views, currently configured as a home office with a queen-size Resource Furniture Murphy bed for seamless guest conversion. A full hall bath with Robern illuminated mirror doubles as a powder room.
Third Bedroom / Flex Room: Park views, twin Milano wall beds by Resource Furniture, and a full en-suite bath with Robern lighted mirror make this room endlessly versatile — guest room, children's room, or creative studio.

Laundry: A dedicated closet with Miele washer and dryer — no trips to the basement, ever.

Additional Features: Individually controlled thermostats in every room, zoned heating and AC, custom-milled HVAC enclosures, and solid oak flooring throughout.

The Building: Designed by George and Edward Blum in 1925 and gut-renovated by S. Russell Groves, 1212 Fifth is the first classic pre-war building in NYC to earn LEED Gold certification. Full-service white-glove living includes a 24-hour attended lobby, concierge, live-in resident manager, residents' lounge, playroom, and 2,600 sq. ft. fitness center. Pool, terrace, screening room, and parking available at adjacent 1214 Fifth Avenue. International buyers, pied-à-terres, and pets welcome — no board approval required.

An architectural masterpiece in the sky, this rare penthouse combination of three residences offers an extraordinary canvas for modern living. With every detail thoughtfully curated, the home itself feels like a work of art—where sweeping views, abundant natural light, and striking interiors converge to create a residence of remarkable beauty.

Spanning 2,725 square feet inside and encircled by a 1,295-square-foot wraparound terrace, the residence is as much an experience as it is a home. Three distinct living rooms and dens invite grand entertaining or intimate retreats, while three oversized bedrooms—with the flexibility to create up to five—redefine what it means to live expansively in Manhattan. Two full kitchens and a separate wet bar add both drama and practicality, making this home equally suited to large gatherings or quiet evenings in.

Throughout, custom-framed windows capture light from every direction—North, South, and East—while bespoke ceiling designs and tailored architectural details elevate each space with a sense of craftsmanship rarely found in modern residences. The views are panoramic and poetic: the shimmer of the East River, the rhythm of the skyline, and endless skies shifting with the city’s energy.

The terrace frames the home with light and air while offering panoramic views of the river, skyline, and open skies. More than an extension of the interiors, it is an architectural feature that defines the residence—an elevated landscape in the heart of Manhattan.

The Royal York provides the perfect frame for such a residence. A full-service Condop with the freedom of condo rules, it offers no board interview, immediate subletting, and is not a land lease. Life here is supported by every amenity: a 24-hour doorman, concierge, fitness center, bike room, storage, garage parking, discounted cable and internet, and half an acre of landscaped gardens—rare breathing room in the heart of the city.

Come home to beloved Carnegie Park, and enjoy every 5 star living luxury Manhattan has to offer. This is a rare and coveted opportunity to combine a 1BR/1B with a 2BR/2B next door. Both 612 & 614 are for sale individually, but this would make possible a combination that would seamlessly create a 4BR/3B. All current floor plans for 612 & 614 and a rendering of the potential combination are procured with this listing.

Carnegie Park truly is a special place to call home. From the fully-landscaped rooftop terrace you can take in the breathtaking 360-degree view of Central Park, the East River and the ever changing Midtown skyline. You can also take advantage of a barbecue grill area.

In the luxurious comfort of both home, you will currently find:

- Desirable loft-like vibes and spectacular oversize panoramic windows flooding the space with light.

- A delightful open Western exposure with wonderful light all day and breathtaking sunsets.

- Custom window treatments.

- Stainless steel kitchen appliances highlighted by Caesarstone countertops.

- Sumptuous bathrooms with marble and custom mosaic details

- Ample closets.

- And the ultimate convenience of a washer/dryer in both units!

The phenomenal and convenient location offers it all in this sought after neighborhood: Central Park, 4/5/6 and Q trains, crosstown bus to the West side, 92nd Street Y, Asphalt Green, post office, museums galore, fine dining, endless shopping (Whole Foods, Target).

Get ready to create a sophisticated one of kind setting for your next chapter. This opportunity will not last, schedule your private showing now!

The Marx Condominiums, a stunning new development, graces Manhattan’s Upper East Side—an iconic neighborhood renowned for its world-class museums, serene parks, high-end shopping, and refined dining. This is quintessential New York City living, where charm and sophistication meet.

Welcome to Unit #2, a full-floor residence with private elevator access. This exquisite home boasts 3 generously sized bedrooms, 3 impeccably designed bathrooms, and a dedicated laundry room. The primary suite is a true sanctuary, complete with garden views where you can unwind and take in the city’s energy from a serene vantage point.

Just a few blocks from Central Park, the Guggenheim Museum, and the Q, 4, 5, and 6 subway lines, The Marx offers unparalleled convenience while maintaining a peaceful retreat on a quiet, tree-lined street. Designed by the esteemed Isaac & Stern Architects, the building’s striking facade—featuring ebonized metal and oversized casement windows—seamlessly merges Art Deco inspiration with modern sensibilities. The architectural design not only reflects the neighborhood’s storied charm but also elevates it with innovative materials and light-filled spaces.

Inside, every detail has been thoughtfully curated. The interiors exude understated elegance, with a palette of pearly whites and earthy neutrals complemented by luxurious white oak flooring, sleek porcelain finishes, and stainless steel accents. The Scandinavian-inspired kitchens are a chef’s dream, equipped with top-tier appliances from brands like Asko, Kohler, and Viking. Bathrooms are equally indulgent, showcasing Kohler fixtures and custom teak millwork.

To top it all off, The Marx features a sprawling rooftop deck, where residents can soak in panoramic city views or host a BBQ in this tranquil escape above the bustling streets of New York City.

The Marx Condominiums redefine luxury living on the Upper East Side—don’t miss the chance to make this extraordinary residence your own.
